observations.recommendation:
For the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 which provides for the One-time Guidelines and Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items;
observations.managementAction: The management is still in the process of implementing the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal Accountant:
b. Exert more effort to come up with a record that will reconcile the Accounting differences with the property custodian/General Service Office (GSO) records;

To Municipal Treasurer:
d. Regularly monitor the status of checks, released and unreleased and send written notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks before it becomes stale;
observations.managementAction: Payees were not notified before the checks become stale.

observations.recommendation:
To Municipal Treasurer:
e. Send written notices to payees on unclaimed checks which were reverted to the liability account for check replacements;
observations.managementAction: Payees of unclaimed checks were not notified.

observations.recommendation:
To Municipal Treasurer:
f. Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks which were unclaimed to the Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transaction as Accounts Payable;
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries were not made.

observations.recommendation:
To Municipal Treasurer:
g. Exhaust all efforts to produce valid supporting documents to make proper adjustments in the books of accounts for all valid reconciling items as against the bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The supporting documents were not retrieved.

observations.recommendation: a. For the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to constituents;

b. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate achievement rate and identify areas with major shortfall or needs improvement in order to make timely and periodic remedial measures and determine the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented projects;
observations.managementAction: Specific timelines to assign in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) were not provided.

observations.recommendation: b. Henceforth, refrain from purchasing high-end or expensive mobile phones and/or expensive electronic gadgets unless justified by circumstances only to conform with the provision set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-003 but primarily to become responsive to the government’s call for judicious and prudent use of government funds and resources.
observations.managementAction: There were still high-end or electronic gadgets not procured.

observations.recommendation: a. For the Municipal Treasurer to declare the whole amount collectible based on the Real Property Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s index and furnish the Accounting Office of the duly certified list of tax payers and their amount due for the year;
a. Provide an explanation and supporting documents for the memorandum, adjusting, and correcting entries made under the General Fund account.
observations.managementAction: There still exist unsubstantiated adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: b. Henceforth, financial transactions should be recognized, recorded, and reported in the books in conformity with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS). Apply the qualitative characteristics of information in financial reporting in order to make the information useful to users and support the achievements of the objectives of financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: Some financial records and reports were still not prepared in an understandable manner which hampers the verification of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
For granting and liquidation of cash advances:
a. For the GSO/Municipal Accountant-Designate to stop performing the disbursing officer’s duties and for operating expenses;
observations.managementAction: GSO and GSO still performs as Special Disbursing Officer for operating expenses.

observations.recommendation: b. Secure completeness of the pertinent documents on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid further audit action and stop granting multiple cash advances to an accountable officer/s if the previous one was not yet fully liquidated; and
observations.managementAction: There were still multiple cash advances granted.

observations.recommendation: Ensure compliance with RA 9184 by adopting competitive bidding, resorting to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases, preparing RFQ and posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.
observations.managementAction: The Management still resort to alternative mode of procurement for most of its purchases.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive is to instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain special account in the General Fund for development projects funded from 20% LDF to facilitate the setting up of the special account as of cut-off date chosen by the Management; identify the continuing appropriations and liabilities under 20% LDF and establish their balances with the equivalent cash account; and thereafter transactions involving the 20% LDF should be recorded under the special account.
observations.managementAction: The Special Account in the General Fund for 20% LDF was still not maintained.

observations.recommendation: a. The management should implement the provisions of Sec. 305 of RA 7160 and endeavor to have a balanced budget in each fiscal year of operation;
observations.reason: Insufficiency of available cash still exists.

observations.recommendation: b. Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee to adopt appropriate measures to control and monitor not only the appropriations and allotments but the cash availability as well. Ensure that 20% LDF, DRRMF, fund transfer from NGAs and other LGUs be utilized for the purpose for which the funds were intended; and
observations.reason: Insufficiency of available cash still exists.

observations.recommendation: c. For the Local Finance Committee to consider different strategies to increase the Municipality’s income and intensify collection and impose cost-cutting measures and prioritize projects and programs.
observations.reason: Insufficiency of available cash still exists.

observations.recommendation: c. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled;
observations.managementAction: The grant of multiple cash advances was still observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that thru (1) Municipal General Services
Officer, to (1.1) develop and implement strategies in the conduct of physical inventory of
PPEs and reconciliation of balances with the Accounting; (1.2) in coordination with the
Accounting Department, investigate and clear all discrepancies between the RPCPPE and
accounting records; and (2) Municipal Accountant to (2.1) devise a plan together with the
GSO to reconcile the differences between the inventory report and the books; and (2.2)
analyse the reconciling items and prepare the necessary journal entries to reflect the
unrecorded PPEs, if any, in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to undertake immediately the disposal
procedures of these unserviceable PPEs taking into consideration the provisions of the
COA Circular 2020-006 or the One-Time Cleansing of PPEs.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) submit a justification for the
procurement of an expensive desktop computer and a copy of the Property
Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) of such item; and (2) refrain from procuring high-end and
expensive items, unless warranted by circumstances, and strictly abide with the laws, rules,
and regulations, otherwise, such transactions will be subject for further audit action.

We recommended and Management agreed that through (1) the Municipal Accountant
to exhaust all efforts to retrieve the supporting documents to substantiate the recording of
journal vouchers to adjust the GL and correct cash balances recognized in the financial
statements, and moving forward; (2) the Municipal Treasurer to (2.1) as a proactive
approach, inform the respective payees of their checks to avoid stale checks; and (2.2)
issue substitute checks in place of the checks that had become stale to settle their
liabilities to these payees.

We recommended and Management agreed to require (1) the Municipal Treasury Office
to prepare a working paper entry for the monthly unreleased checks which shall not be
recorded/entered in the books of accounts every month however may facilitate a complete
record of unreleased checks at the end of the year; (2) moving forward, ensure that the
contact information of the payees are on record to facilitate the timely release of these
checks; and (3) the Municipal Accountant, at every end of the year, to draw JEV to record
the entry for the restoration of cash equivalent to the unreleased checks and recognition of
the appropriate payable/liability accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Accountant to establish
the breakdown component of every major real account reflected in the financial
statements and for this purpose, prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers. Moreover,
we recommend the Municipal Accountant to review and be guided by the NGAS Manual
for LGUs and other COA issuances for the procedures and appropriate accounting
formats to be used in recording the financial transactions of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to impose stringent internal control
measures in the disbursement process by strictly complying with the prohibitions of the
pertinent guidelines for future disbursements and avoiding the incurrence of disbursements
not covered by an appropriation ordinance, otherwise, such transactions will be subject to
disallowance.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) stop granting additional cash
advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or
settled; (2) ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific
purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents; and (3) impose
stringent internal control in the grant and liquidation of cash advances by strictly adhering to
the guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 1997-02 on the grant, utilization, liquidation of
cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) justify why the designated Municipal
Administrator signed and approved non-recurring disbursements of the LGU and moving
forward; and (2) comply with the provisions of R.A. 7160 on the approval of vouchers and/or
stop the practice of approving payment of non-recurring obligations and claims inconsistent
with pertinent rules and regulation, to avoid further audit action on the transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) include in the MOA with barangays
the clause on the period of implementation of projects and schedule of submission of
liquidation; and (2) sufficiently carry out detailed engineering investigations, surveys and
designs before bidding and award of contract in relation to Section 17.6 of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed that Municipal Accounting Office strictly
ensure that all disbursements from the government funds are duly supported with
complete documentary requirements in adherence to COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Municipal Accounting Office to (1)
to draw an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous charging of various transactions from the
Local Development Fund, and thereafter exercise due diligence in the recording of
transactions to ensure proper charging of expenditures from its appropriate fund source in
strict adherence with the Annual Investment Program in the utilization of the 20 percent
development fund, and (2) identify the total continuing appropriations and liabilities under
the 20 percent Development Fund and establish their balances with the equivalent cash
account. Thereafter, create and maintain a special account for the 20 percent
development fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to (1) submit the lacking documentary
requirements to assess the validity, legality, and propriety of the disbursements charged
against DRRMF; (2) prepare a JEV for the recognition of the PPE accounts and
accumulated depreciation, as well as to correct Prior-Year; and (3) moving forward,
perform a thorough review of all disbursement claims to ensure that these are properly
supported with complete documentation and recorded in its appropriate accounts.

For the Municipal Accountant:
c. Provide the Fully depreciation expenses for all the properties and recognize it in the books of accounts by preparing an adjusting journal entry. In case of absence of records as basis for the computation, assess whether there is indication for impairment and recognize impairment loss, if warranted. After the recognition of impairment loss, compute the depreciation charge for the properties using the revised carrying amount, less its residual value, on a systematic basis over its remaining useful life or revised estimated useful life;

For the Municipal Accountant:
d. Reclassify the semi-expandable items with costs not exceeding ₱15,000.00 to expense in accordance with the guidelines provided in Local Government Accounting Manual (LGAM);

For the Municipal General Service Officer:
e. Strictly adhere to conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor;
observations.managementAction: Various items were still not included in the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation:
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
f. Ensure that all significant information required in the RPCPPE are completely provided to fast track the reconciliation process with the accounting records.
AAR g. Direct the MAO to maintain property ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation and recognition of depreciation expenses.

To Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare completely the bank reconciliation statements on a monthly basis for all cash in bank accounts maintained by the Municipality and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries on the noted differences and stale checks;

To Municipal Accountant:
b. Strictly adhere to the usage of the forms provided under the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, with regards the preparation of Ledgers;
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers were not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
To Municipal Accountant:
c. Draw a journal entry to transfer the Cash-in-Bank Accounts held in Trust from the General Fund to the Trust Fund account;
a. Ensure proper classification of accounts in adherence to COA Circular 2015-009 to present a fair and verifiable Financial Position of the Municipality;

b. Draw an adjusting journal entry to reclassify the non-current liability of the loan from Land Bank of the Philippines, recognize the Interest Payable in the books of the Municipality, recognize liquidations for fully completed projects and transfer of the fund received for specific purpose to Trust Fund; and

c. Strictly adhere the proper recording of funds received for specific purpose to Trust Fund accounts, maintain and monitor the cash availability of funds to cover the implementation of specific projects in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 7160 and P.D. 1445.

a. The Municipal Accounting Office and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) shall immediately submit the lacking documents such as the Mayor’s/Business Permit, Omnibus Sworn Statement, Driver’s Trip Ticket and Fuel Consumption Report, Official Receipt/Sales Invoice, and other necessary supporting documents, otherwise, it will be subjected to the issuance of Notice of Suspension. Also, the Municipal Accountant shall check the completeness of the necessary documentary requirements for the procurement are complete, valid and legal before payment is made;

b. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) or designated persons in-charge of procurement activities shall be updated and acquaint themselves with the latest GPPB issuances to ensure complete and strict compliance with the prescribed procedures and documentation. Also, strictly implement the required procedures and proper documentation as well as timing in the submission of vital documents such as Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) to evade the possible risks associated with procurement activities;

c. The concerned departments shall indicate the necessary data required such as the dates in the Purchase Request, Notice of Award, and Purchase Orders as well as the date of receipt of the Supplier or Contractor to ensure the efficiency of procurement activities as well as the performance of delivery as basis in the computation of the liquidated damages in case of delay in the delivery of procurement;
d. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat shall consolidate the PPMP of each office and prepare Annual and Supplemental Procurement Plan; and

e. The BAC Secretariat shall ensure full compliance with the reporting and posting requirements in the GPPB online portal and LGU website or full disclosure boards. Henceforth, the Audit Team advise the Management to adhere the reportorial and posting requirements for any similar situation not only to promote transparency and accountability but also to help any concerned agency in the timely data gathering and consolidation of reports that will be useful in the decision-making process of the rightful authorities.

a. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Budget Officer shall strictly implement the guidelines on the payment of regular hazard pay and COVID-19 hazard pay, as well as other additional benefits granted in response to COVID-19 pandemic, to ensure the validity and accuracy of payment;

b. Submit the lacking documents to assert the propriety, validity and legality of the payment of COVID-19 Hazard Pay such as the Daily time Records (DTR), Certification from the Head of the Office and approved Work Schedule, for evaluation in audit, otherwise, this will be subjected to the issuance of Notice of Suspension; and

c. Strictly implement the guidelines on disbursements and immediately stop incurring disbursements in excess of appropriation pursuant to Section 305 (a) of R.A. No. 7160. Further, stop granting additional bonuses and incentives that are devoid of legal basis and ensure that all approved disbursements are supported by sufficient appropriations.
c. For the Municipal Accountant to verify the recording of the fund transfer and liquidations, and coordinate with the Barangay Treasurer to reconcile the noted variances; and

d. For the Local Budget Officer to determine the status of excess funds from completed projects and ensure that the aforementioned funds were free from any encumbrances and recommend to the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian Bayan for its reversion and re-appropriate it to other development projects of the LGU.
a. Direct the key personnel to submit justification on why the procurement of the eight (8) mobile phones totaling ₱624,000.00 shall not be disallowed in audit, for evaluation;

To Municipal Accountant:
a. Observe the use of Perpetual Inventory Method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual in recording the Inventory account;

To Municipal Accountant:
b. Record properly all purchases to its appropriate inventory account and maintain a supply ledger card for each type of inventory;

To Municipal General Service Officer:
c. Maintain stock cards for supplies in your custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and

To Municipal General Service Officer:
d. Prepare Monthly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (MSSMI) duly supported by Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from end-users for submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances of inventories.

b. For the Municipal Accountant to properly set-up the balance of the SET receivable at the beginning of the year and its corresponding deferred credit and record the said receivables account and its related account based on the RPTAR/Tax Index Card report from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

a. For granting and liquidation of overtime services: For the Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Budget Officer: Provide compensatory time/days-off, in lieu of the overtime pay, or non-compensatory remedial actions as per CSC and DBM JC No. 2 s. 2004;
b. Strictly adhere to the provision of Section 305 (a) of RA 7160 by avoiding disbursements in excess of appropriation;
c. Stop the practice of granting overtime pay to the department heads and assistant department heads in compliance with Item 6.0 of CSC and DBM JC No. 1 s. 2015;

d. Formulate a detailed and systematic plan of activities per department. The planned activities shall integrate therewith the volume and frequency of regular and routine tasks to be performed to ensure that overtime services will be avoided; and

e. Require the Municipal Accounting Office in coordination with the Human Resource Management Office to ensure that withholding taxes in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table are deducted from payments made in accordance with the above-mentioned regulations and that the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.
c. Submit the lacking OBRs, POs, PRs and other pertinent documents for those recorded as liquidated cash advances otherwise, these will be subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
a. With the aid of necessary documents, support and ensure reconciliation with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of unsettled receivable due from GOCC;

a. For the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant Designate to maintain General Ledgers due to LGUs and Subsidiary Ledgers, not only for Due to LGUs but also for all other accounts for all funds, in order to support and ensure validity and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements;
b. Henceforth, abide with the provisions of RA 7160 in maintaining Trust Fund account for funds that came into the possession of the LGU for the implementation of specific projects.
With the aid of necessary documents, reconcile records with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of unsettled receivable due from GOCC.

a. Strengthen the LGU’s internal control system through planning of organization, the segregation of custodial and accounting functions and installation of coordinated measures and procedures i.e. proper documentation, and proper procurement and use of the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System;
observations.reason: Deficiencies in procurement process were still noted.

observations.recommendation: b. The GSO/Designated Municipal Accountant and MBO to refrain from performing the disbursing duties as the custodial, recording and reporting functions are incompatible and should be segregated and assigned to separate individuals to maintain principle of check and balance in the agency;
observations.managementAction: The GSO still performs as Special Disbursing Officer for operating expenses.

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386.
observations.reason: Some parcels of land were still not transferred under the name of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
a. For the Municipal Treasurer to declare the whole amount collectible based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s index and furnish Accounting Office of the duly certified list of taxpayers, their amount due and collectible for the year;
b. For the Municipal Accountant to properly set-up the balance of the SET receivable at the beginning of the year and its corresponding deferred credit and record the said receivables account and its related account based on the RPTAR/Tax Index Card report from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer. Further, basis of recording the beginning balance of the Real Property Tax Receivable was not in accordance with Sec. 20 of NGAS Manual.

a. Ensure compliance with RA 9184 by adopting competitive bidding, resorting to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional cases, preparing RFQ and posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.
b. For BAC and Accounting to ensure all government transactions are duly supported with complete documentary requirements under COA Circular 2012-001 and other existing issuances of COA.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following actions to
improve the financial reporting of the PPE accounts:
For the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply with the
provisions of COA Circular 2020-006 which provides for the Guidelines and
Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition
for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE
Account Balances of Government Agencies;
For the Municipal Accountant:
2. Exert more effort to come up with a record that will reconcile the differences
with the property custodian/General Service Office (GSO) records;
3. Provide the depreciation expenses for all the properties and recognize them in
the books of accounts by preparing an adjusting journal entry. In case of
absence of records as a basis for the computation, assess whether there is an
indication for impairment and recognize an impairment loss, if warranted5.
After the recognition of impairment loss, compute the depreciation charge for
the properties using the revised carrying amount, less its residual value, on a
systematic basis over its remaining useful life or revised estimated useful life6;
4. Reclassify the semi-expandable items with costs not exceeding ₱15,000.00 to
expense in accordance with the guidelines provided in the Local Government
Accounting Manual (LGAM);
For the Municipal General Service Officer:
5. Strictly adhere to conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report
on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every
January 31 of each year to the Office of the Auditor; and
6. Ensure that all significant information required in the RPCPPE is completely
provided to fast-track the reconciliation process with the accounting records.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following actions:
To Municipal Accountant:
1. Prepare completely the bank reconciliation statements every month for all
cash in bank accounts maintained by the Municipality and effect the necessary
adjusting journal entries on the noted differences and stale checks;
2. Strictly adhere to the usage of the forms provided under the Manual on NGAS
for LGUs, Volume II 10 with regards to the preparation of Ledgers;
3. Draw a journal entry to transfer the Cash-in-Bank Accounts held in Trust
from the General Fund to the Trust Fund account;
To Municipal Treasurer:
4. Regularly monitor the status of checks, released and unreleased and send
written notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks
before it becomes stale;
5. Send written notices to payees on unclaimed checks which were reverted to the
liability account for check replacements;
6. Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks which
were unclaimed to the Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the transaction as Accounts Payable; and
7. Exhaust all efforts to produce valid supporting documents to make proper
adjustments in the books of accounts for all valid reconciling items as against
the bank accounts.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the
following courses of actions:
1. Ensure proper classification of accounts in adherence to COA Circular 2015-
009 to present a fair and verifiable Financial Position of the Municipality;
2. Draw an adjusting journal entry to reclassify the non-current liability of the
loan from Land Bank of the Philippines, recognize the Interest Payable in the
books of the Municipality, recognize liquidations for fully completed projects
and transfer the fund received for a specific purpose to Trust Fund; and
3. Strictly adhere to the proper recording of funds received for a specific purpose
to Trust Fund accounts, maintain and monitor the cash availability of funds
to cover the implementation of specific projects in accordance with the
provisions of R.A. 7160 and P.D. 1445.

With the foregoing observation, we recommended and Management agreed on
the following:
1. The Municipal Accounting Office and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
shall immediately submit the lacking documents such as the Mayor’s/Business
Permit, Omnibus Sworn Statement, Driver’s Trip Ticket, and Fuel
Consumption Report, Official Receipt/Sales Invoice, and other necessary
supporting documents, otherwise, it will be subjected to the issuance of Notice
of Suspension. Also, the Municipal Accountant shall check the completeness
of the necessary documentary requirements for the procurement are complete,
valid, and legal before payment is made;
2. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) or designated persons in charge of
procurement activities shall be updated and acquaint themselves with the
latest GPPB issuances to ensure complete and strict compliance with the
prescribed procedures and documentation. Also, strictly implement the
required procedures and proper documentation as well as timing in the
submission of vital documents such as Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) to
evade the possible risks associated with procurement activities;
3. The concerned departments shall indicate the necessary data required such as
the dates in the Purchase Request, Notice of Award, and Purchase Orders as
well as the date of receipt of the Supplier or Contractor to ensure the efficiency
of procurement activities as well as the performance of delivery as a basis in
the computation of the liquidated damages in case of delay in the delivery of
procurement;
4. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat shall consolidate the
PPMP of each office and prepare Annual and Supplemental Procurement
Plan; and
5. The BAC Secretariat shall ensure full compliance with the reporting and
posting requirements in the GPPB online portal and LGU website or full
disclosure boards. Henceforth, the Audit Team advises the Management to
adhere to the reportorial and posting requirements for any similar situation
not only to promote transparency and accountability but also to help any
concerned agency in the timely data gathering and consolidation of reports
that will be useful in the decision-making process of the rightful authorities.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the
following:
1. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant, Human Resource Management
Officer, and Municipal Budget Officer shall strictly implement the guidelines
on the payment of regular hazard pay and COVID-19 hazard pay, as well as
other additional benefits granted in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, to
ensure the validity and accuracy of payment;
2. Submit the lacking documents to assert the propriety, validity, and legality of
the payment of COVID-19 Hazard Pay such as the Daily time Records (DTR),
Certification from the Head of the Office, and approved Work Schedule, for
evaluation in audit, otherwise, this will be subjected to the issuance of Notice
of Suspension; and
3. Strictly implement the guidelines on disbursements and immediately stop
incurring disbursements more than appropriation pursuant to Section 305 (a)
of R.A. No. 7160. Further, stop granting additional bonuses and incentives that
are devoid of legal basis and ensure that all approved disbursements are
supported by sufficient appropriations.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed on the
following courses of actions:
1. For the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to
identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development
projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal
utilization of funds and timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to
constituents;
2. For the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign
specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate
achievement rate and identify areas with a major shortfall or needs
improvement to make timely and periodic remedial measures and determine
the feasibility of implementation of prior year’s unimplemented projects;
3. For the Municipal Accountant to verify the recording of the fund transfer and
liquidations, and coordinate with the Barangay Treasurer to reconcile the
noted variances; and
4. For the Local Budget Officer to determine the status of excess funds from
completed projects and ensure that the aforementioned funds were free from
any encumbrances and recommend to the Local Chief Executive and
Sanggunian Bayan for its reversion and re-appropriate it to other
development projects of the LGU.

We recommended and Management agreed for the Local Chief Executive to:
1. Direct the key personnel to submit justification on why the procurement of the
eight (8) mobile phones totaling ₱624,000.00 shall not be disallowed in audit,
for evaluation; and
2. Henceforth, refrain from purchasing high-end or expensive mobile phones
and/or electronic gadgets unless justified by circumstances not only to conform
with the provision set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-003 but primarily
to become responsive to the government’s call for judicious and prudent use
of government funds and resources.

Adopt other measure such as requesting for monthly snapshot from the bank and follow up the enrollment to LBP’s “weAccess” online viewing facility to facilitate timely preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements.
For granting and liquidation of multiple cash advances:
a. The Local Chief Executive should give priority and immediately hire a full-time Municipal Accountant, a CPA to occupy the mandatory position of the LGU.
b. Strictly comply to the requirements of Section 474 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in order to provide and submit an accurate and reliable reports of the agency and to enforce compliance to the existing internal control system.
c. Submit an explanation/justification of the deferment/non-hiring of a CPA which is a mandatory position of the LGU.
d. For the GSO/Municipal Accountant-Designate to stop from performing the duties of disbursing officer’s duties and responsibilities;
e. Secure completeness of the pertinent documents on the granting and liquidation of cash advances to avoid further audit action and stop granting additional cash advances to an usually accountable officer/s if the previous one was not yet fully liquidated; and
f. Submit the lacking OBRs, POs, PRs and other pertinent documents for those recorded as liquidated cash advances otherwise, these will be subject to the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

Adopt other measure such as requesting for monthly snapshot from the bank and follow up the enrollment to LBP’s “weAccess” online viewing facility to facilitate timely preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements.

With the aid of necessary documents, reconcile records with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of receivable due from them. A final demand letter was sent to Malungon Water District and partial payments were collected for the year 2020.
The Local Chief Executive should ensure compliance with RA 7160 by:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to strictly comply with Section 9 and 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs on the maintenance of General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers, not only for Due to LGUs but also for all other accounts for all funds, in order to support and ensure validity and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to transfer the liabilities to source agencies to Trust Fund including its corresponding cash account and follow proper account classification, e.g., “Due to NGAs” for fund transfers from National Government Agencies and “Due to LGUs” for fund transfers from the Provincial Government, by making the following adjustments:
General Fund:

Cash 32,967,459.28

Trust Fund:
Cash 32,967,459.28
Due to NGAs 32,863,655.01
Due to LGUs 103,804.27

c. Henceforth, abide with the provisions of RA 7160 in maintaining Trust Fund account for funds that came into the possession of the LGU for the implementation of specific projects.

With the aid of necessary documents, reconcile records with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of receivable due from them. A final demand letter was sent to Malungon Water District and partial payments were collected for the year 2020.
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) is to direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to ensure that the projects to be funded by the LDF, especially by re-alignment, are derived from the local development plans and AIP. In case all PPAs included in the AIP were already funded, a supplemental AIP should be prepared which will be the basis in funding additional PPAs.
The Local Chief Executive is to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the augmentation and re-alignment are strictly followed.
The Local Chief Executive is to instruct the Municipal Accountant Designate to maintain special account in the General Fund for development projects funded from the 20% of the IRA. To facilitate the setting up of the special account as of cut-off date chosen by the Management, the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Budget Officer should identify continuing appropriations and liabilities under 20% and establish their balances with the equivalent cash account. Henceforth, transactions involving the 20% LDF should be recorded under the special account.
With the aid of necessary documents, reconcile records with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of receivable due from them.
observations.managementAction: A final demand letter was sent to Malungon Water District and partial payments were collected for the year 2020.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive shall ensure compliance with the provisions of Section 2 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386. (Recommendations: continuous follow-up for titling and registration as necessary.)
observations.managementAction: Continuous follow-up of the Municipal Assessor who has been delegated and tasked on the matter.

observations.recommendation:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to strictly comply with Section 9 and 10 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs on the maintenance of General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers, not only for Due to LGUs but also for all other accounts for all funds, in order to support and ensure validity and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to transfer the liabilities to source agencies to Trust Fund including its corresponding cash account and follow proper account classification, e.g. under “Due to NGAs” for fund transfers from National Government Agencies and “Due to LGUs” for fund transfers from the Provincial Government, by making the following adjustments:
General Fund:
Due to LGUs 32,967,459.28
Cash 32,967,459.28
To record reclassification of fund transfers from source agencies under General Fund to Trust Fund.

Trust Fund:
Cash 32,967,459.28
Due to NGAs 32,863,655.01
Due to LGUs 103,804.27
To record reclassification of fund transfers from source agencies under General Fund to Trust Fund.

3. Henceforth, abide with the provisions of RA 7160 in maintaining Trust Fund account for funds that came into the possession of the LGU for the implementation of specific projects.

A. Appropriation and Utilization
1. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) is to direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to ensure that the projects to be funded by the LDF, especially by re-alignment, are derived from the local development plans and that PPAs are included in the AIP. In case all PPAs included in the AIP were already funded, a supplemental AIP should be prepared which will be the basis in funding additional PPAs.
2. The Local Chief Executive is to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the augmentation and re-alignment are strictly followed.
3. The Local Chief Executive is to instruct the Municipal Accountant Designate to maintain special account in the General Fund for development projects funded from the 20% of the IRA. To facilitate the setting up of the special account as of cut-off date chosen by the Management, the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer should cause first the identification of the continuing appropriations and liabilities under 20% LDF and establish their balances with the equivalent cash account. Henceforth, transactions involving the 20% LDF should be recorded under the special fund. Through this, Management can start to maintain the special account for 20% LDF mandated by law and required by sound financial management.

Henceforth, avoid lump-sum appropriations, instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity, and project.

The BAC Secretariat should comply with the requirements under RA 9184 by posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.

1. Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386.
2. Seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the issuance of Deed of Assignment over all areas owned by different agencies.

1. Adopt other measure such as requesting for monthly snapshot from the bank or enrollment to LBP’s “weAccess” online viewing facility to facilitate timely preparation and submission of bank reconciliation statements; 2. The designate Municipal Accountant should seek assistance of the depository banks to recapture necessary information and determine the validity of the long outstanding reconciling items for thereby leaving the appropriate adjustments in the books; 3. The designate Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entry voucher for the cancellation and reversion of stale checks. 4. Monitor regularly the cash balances and transactions to determine proper use of the accounts. Close depository accounts in which the purpose had already been satisfied to avoid possible dormancy charges or below maintaining balance bank charges; 5. Henceforth, the BRS submitted to the COA Auditor should be supported by copies of the bank statements, JEV recording reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts, as well as a copy of the letter to the respective bank informing them of errors noted in the BRS (other than those resulting from the timing of recording of transactions).
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017

observations.recommendation: 1. The management should implement the provisions of Sec. 305 of RA 7160 and endeavor to have a balanced budget in each fiscal year of operation; 2. Ensure that all disbursements are supported with Disbursement Vouchers and Obligation Requests and duly certified and approved by proper signatories; 3. Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee to adopt appropriate measures to control and monitor not only the appropriations and allotments but the cash availability as well. Ensure that 20% LDF, DRRMF, fund transfer from NGAs and other LGUs be utilized for the purpose for which the funds were intended; and 4. For the Local Finance Committee to consider different strategies to increase the Municipality’s income and intensify collection and impose cost-cutting measures and prioritize projects and programs.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: The BAC Secretariat should comply with the requirements under RA 9184 by posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and reiterated and Management agreed on the following:
a. Direct the Inventory Committee to strategize the conduct of complete
physical inventory taking of all the assets and properties of the LGU and
ensure that items in RPCPPE are classified according to PPE type guided by
the PPSAS-prescribed classification to facilitate easier reconciliation with
the MAO;
b. Direct the MAO to maintain property ledger cards to facilitate the
reconciliation and recognition of depreciation expenses;
c. Direct the Inventory Committee to ensure that the reconciliation process
between the physical inventory records of the MGSO and accounting
records are properly in placed, to come up with a reliable report.

a. Adopt other measure such as requesting for monthly snapshot from the bank
and follow up the enrollment to LBP’s “weAccess” online viewing facility to
facilitate timely preparation and submission of bank reconciliation
statements;
b. Recapture necessary information and determine the purpose of long
outstanding bank debit and credit memo. If found valid, prepare necessary
adjusting entry in the books;
c. Prepare journal entry voucher for the cancellation and reversion of stale
checks and recognition of receivable from supplier due to overpayment;
d. Henceforth, prepare a correct BRS and submit to the COA Auditor a copy of
BRS supported by copies of the bank statements, JEV recording reconciling
items that require correction in the books of accounts, as well as a copy of the
letter to the respective bank informing them of errors noted in the BRS (other
than those resulting from the timing of recording of transactions).

for all other accounts for all funds, in order to support and ensure validity
and accuracy of the balances reflected in the financial statements.
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to transfer the liabilities to
source agencies to Trust Fund including its corresponding cash account
and follow proper account classification, e.g. “Due to NGAs” for fund
transfers from National Government Agencies and “Due to LGUs” for fund
transfers from the Provincial Government, by making the following
adjustments:
General Fund:
Due to LGUs 32,967,459.28
Cash 32,967,459.28
To record reclassification of fund transfers from source agencies under
General Fund to Trust Fund.
Trust Fund:
Cash 32,967,459.28
Due to NGAs 32,863,655.01
Due to LGUs 103,804.27
To record reclassification of fund transfers from source agencies under
General Fund to Trust Fund.
c. Henceforth, abide with the provisions of RA 7160 in maintaining Trust
Fund account for funds that came into the possession of the LGU for the
implementation of specific projects.

For BAC to:
a. Require each department/office to prepare PPMP for all PPAs for their
respective departments/offices requirements;
b. Consolidate all Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) into the
Annual Procurement Plan (APP);
c. Ensure compliance with RA 9184 by adopting competitive bidding,
resorting to alternative methods of procurement only in highly exceptional
cases, preparing RFQ and posting the invitation or request for submission
of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the
PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.
For BAC and Accounting to:
d. Ensure all government transactions are duly supported with complete
documentary requirements under COA Circular 2012-001 and other
existing issuances of COA.

General Fund for development projects funded from the 20% of the IRA. To facilitate
the setting up of the special account as of cut-off date chosen by the Management,

observations.recommendation: Increase LGU’s collection efficiency of GSIS loans on the succeeding years.

observations.recommendation: 1. The LCE, to instruct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) to ensure that the projects to be funded by the LDF shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environment management outcomes and take the nature of investment or capital expenditures as set in DILG_DBM JMC No. 2017-1; 2. The LCE, to direct to stop the use of LDF to pay out expenses that are not related to and/or not connected with the implementation of development PPAs, such as honoraria, training, traveling, office supplies, office equipment, etc; 3. Henceforth, avoid lump-sum appropriations, instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity, and project; and 4. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 relative to the documentary requirements for each type of disbursement.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 1. The MAO to maintain ledger cards and provide details of the PPE accounts to facilitate the reconciliation and recognition of depreciation expenses; 2. The Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties in accordance with existing regulations in order to establish actual existence; 3. The GSO to maintain complete and updated property cards and timely submit updated RPCPPE to the accounting office to facilitate reconciliation with the latter’s property ledger and make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balance of the PPE accounts in the financial statements; and 4. The MAO to provide the details and identify the projects under the CIP accounts to facilitate proper classification. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) to identify whether or not the projects have been completed and transfer the CIP record of completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts and the related depreciation expense thereat should also be computed and properly recorded. 5. Make appropriate and necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 1. Locate documents such as Local Budget Memorandum and/or Memorandum of Agreements and furnish the audit team a copy to support the validity of claims from NGAs and GOCCs; 2. With the aid of necessary documents, reconcile records with the concerned agencies and cause the collection of receivable due from them; 3. Also with the aid of necessary documents, properly identify the classification of receivables and prepare necessary adjusting entries; 4. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts and request for write-off of dormant accounts; and 5. Monitor compliance on the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period and grant additional fund assistance only when the previous ones are settled. Also, improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation of fund releases.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: 1. Strengthen the LGU’s internal control system through planning of organization, segregation of custodial and accounting functions and installation of coordinated measures and procedures i.e. proper documentation, and proper procurement and use of the Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System; 2. The GSO/Designated Municipal Accountant and MBO to refrain from performing the duties of disbursing officer as the custodial, recording and reporting functions are incompatible and should be segregated and assigned to separate individuals to maintain principle of check and balance in the agency; 3. Adhere with provisions in granting and liquidation of cash advances, hence ensure that cash advances shall only be given for a legally authorized specific purpose and adequately supported with relevant supporting documents; 4. Stop granting additional cash advances to officials and employees until the previous ones have been liquidated and/or settled; 5. Submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and liquidation vouchers/reports as well as its supporting documents, otherwise reconstruct the balance of outstanding cash advances and reinstate previously recorded liquidation not duly supported by necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: 1. Enforce the policy on waste segregation as mandated under RA 9003; 2. Expedite the construction of a sanitary landfill; 3. Provide safety gears to the people assigned in waste collection.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: The LGU should maximize the implementation of the various projects, programs and activities under the 20% Local Development Fund that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.

observations.reason: No response or action yet from DENR. Initial budget was appropriated for the processing of papers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Enforce the policy on waste segregation as mandated under RA 9003;
Expedite the construction of a sanitary landfill; and
Provide safety gears to the people assigned in waste collection.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Secretariat comply with the requirements under RA 9184 by posting the invitation or request for submission of price quotation, as well as the other required documents, in the PhilGEPS website and the Procuring Entity's website and premises.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
Maximize the utilization of available resources to achieve utmost disaster
preparedness; and
Prepare LDRRMFIP that includes current and previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF to maximize the programs, projects and activities relating to mitigation, prevention and preparedness.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU maximize the utilization of the various projects, programs and activities under the 20% Local Development Fund that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting and General Services Offices reconcile their records to reflect the correct balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that all the planned programs, projects and activities incorporated in the LGU’s budget should be included in the Annual Investment Program. Revisit the guidelines set on the harmonization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration, budgeting and expenditure management in the DBM-NEDA-DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007.

We recommended and Management agreed that all programs and activities that are to be implemented by the LGU should contain specific as to the amount, name, nature and place to be undertaken. Henceforth, lump sum appropriation should be avoided. Instead, ensure that the local budget operationalizes the approved local development plans and contains identified objects of expenditures for each function, activity and project.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LGU maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, program and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20%Development Fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accounting Office establish and maintain a special account for the transactions under the 20% Local Development Fund.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC prepare and submit the PMR to the GPPB. Aside from compliance with rules and regulations in the submission of the PMR, the Local Chief Executive, through the BAC, should make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement process of the Municipality.

Strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 2 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386.

Appropriate funds to defray necessary expenses pertinent with the registration and titling of the real estate occupied by the local government offices.
observations.managementAction:
Letter request was sent to DENR for the required documentations and processing of papers for the titling of lands.

Funds were set aside for the expenses.
observations.reason:
No response or action yet from DENR.

Initial budget was appropriated for the processing of papers.
